The Universal Pictures Globe DISSOLVES into a GOLD DISC, embossed with a gold SCORPION. The gold disc is the masthead at the top of a banner being carried by an AKKADIAN SOLDIER. Behind the Sumerians is the massive walled CITY OF THEBES: palaces, temples, needles, --the works. The Nile runs alongside it.
Six thousand years ago, a fierce warrior known as the Scorpion King, led a great army on a campaign to unite the known world.

A powerful FI.ST is thrust into FRAME, clenching a scimitar, a GOLD BRACELET adorns the warrior's wrist. Muscular, brutal looking and heavily scarred, this is the SCORPION KING. The spooky thing about him is that through a strange Akkadian tribal ritual, all the skin on his head above his eyebrows has bE!en remoyad. exposing his BLEACHED SKULLCAP. He BELLOWS a command. He and his soldiers CHARGE FORWARD. CUT WIDE: as both armies, fifteen thousand men strong, pour over the golden dunes and CLASH. The Scorpion King fights like a man possessed. Slowly being swallowed up in the dust.
The Scorpion King and his army were finally defeated by the Sumerians and driven deep into the sacred desert of Ahm Shere.
